EPISODE_NARRATIVE: High pressure was located over much of the eastern United States for a consecutive day and light southerly flow persisted all week which led to above normal temperatures and dew points in the mid 70s.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heat indices of around 110 were measured at Reagan National.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: High pressure was anchored off shore while low pressure spun over the Great Lakes. A shortwave trough moved over the region initiating showers and thunderstorms in vicinity of a surface trough.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: There was a tree down on 295 near Malcolm X Ave.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: High pressure was anchored off shore while low pressure spun over the Great Lakes.  A shortwave trough moved over the region initiating showers and thunderstorms in vicinity of a surface trough.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: There was a car stuck in flood waters in a South Capitol Street underpass.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: High pressure was anchored off shore while low pressure spun over the Great Lakes.  A shortwave trough moved over the region initiating showers and thunderstorms in vicinity of a surface trough.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: South Capitol Street was flooded and closed at Interstate 295.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A frontal boundary moved northward and stalled over the Mid-Atlantic/I-95 corridor overnight. An upper level low approached the region and a upper level jet added to enhanced lift in the region. Heavy rain caused a lot of flash flooding with amounts up to seven inches in less than 6 hours recorded near Washington Dulles Airport.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Canal Road was closed due to flooding.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A shortwave trough moved north of the region. Organized convection developed near the I-95 corridor where instability was the greatest. Scattered showers and thunderstorms produced large hail, heavy rainfall and damaging winds.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: There was water covering on south bound Capitol Street SE and Malcolm X Ave SE at the Overpass which was closed.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: High pressure was wedged down the east coast and surface temperatures fell below freezing. Low pressure developed across the gulf coast states and moved across the Ohio Valley. Snow began but changed over to sleet and freezing rain as warmer temperatures aloft overran the cold air at the surface.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Snow accumulations of two inches and ice accumulations of around a quarter inch fell at Dalecarlia Reservoir and surrounding locations.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A cold front stalled across the Mid Atlantic and low pressure formed and moved along it. Southerly flow advected gulf moisture into the region and showers and thunderstorms easily formed. Heavy rain, hail, damaging winds and tornadoes occurred throughout the day.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A tree fell onto a car at Willard Road and 18th NW.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: High pressure was located over much of the eastern United States and light southerly flow persisted all week which led to above normal temperatures and dew points in the mid 70s.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heat indices were around 105 to 109 degrees at the Reagan National Airport.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A fast moving upper level disturbance and jet max moved across the region and mid level convergence led to snow banding across Northern Maryland. Banding led to 1-2 inches an hour in some locations.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Snow accumulations of around 2 inches was measured at Anacostia.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An upper level disturbance moved ahead of an approaching cold front. Southerly flow ahead of system led to increased moisture and warmer temperatures. There was enough instability to produce gusty winds, heavy rain and a tornado.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: There was a tree down blocking Loughboro Road.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An upper level disturbance moved ahead of an approaching cold front. Southerly flow ahead of system led to increased moisture and warmer temperatures. There was enough instability to produce gusty winds, heavy rain and a tornado.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: There was a large tree branch down on Raum Street.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: High pressure was centered off the Atlantic coast.  Southerly flow has transported mild temperatures and moisture northward into the Mid Atlantic Region.  Calm winds and low level moisture led to dense fog overnight.  This was the second consecutive day of dense fog in the region.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Visibility was reported around a quarter mile at Reagan National Airport.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A stacked low pressure system was over the Northern Plains.  High pressure to the north caused below freezing temperatures to advect southward into the Mid Atlantic.  Southerly flow aloft and ahead of low pressure led to above freezing temperatures above the below freezing surface.  Freezing rain occurred in most areas in the Mid Atlantic.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Ice amounts of around a trace were reported at surrounding locations.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: High pressure was centered off the Atlantic coast. Southerly flow has transported mild temperatures and moisture northward into the Mid Atlantic Region. Calm winds and low level moisture led to dense fog overnight. This was the third consecutive day of dense fog in the region.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Visibility was reported around a quarter mile at Reagan National Airport.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A clipper system moved through the Mid Atlantic in the early morning hours and produced advisory level snowfall in the Baltimore and Washington DC metro areas.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Snowfall amounts of around 1 inch were reported at surrounding observations during the morning rush hour.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Coastal low pressure impacted the Mid Atlantic region with snow and rain showers.  Surface temperatures were marginal during the event and a sharp gradient of snowfall accumulation existed in the vicinity of Washington DC.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Snowfall totaled up to 2.5 inches at American University.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A frontal boundary moved northward and stalled over the Mid-Atlantic/I-95 corridor overnight. An upper level low approached the region and a upper level jet added to enhanced lift in the region. Heavy rain caused a lot of flash flooding with amounts up to seven inches in less than 6 hours recorded near Washington Dulles Airport.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Virginia Ave NW was closed between 22nd and 24th due to flash flooding.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: High pressure was slow to move off the Atlantic Coast and sub freezing air was trapped on the leeward side of the Appalachian Mountains.  Southwest flow a few thousand feet above the surface brought above freezing temperatures and led to freezing rain and accumulating ice in the Mid Atlantic.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Ice accumulation of around a trace was reported at Chevy Chase and surrounding locations.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An Alberta clipper moved through the Mid Atlantic producing light snow for most of the region.  Dry air at the surface limited snowfall amounts for most of the area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Snowfall amounts of around 1 inch were reported at surrounding locations during the morning rush hour.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A positively tilted trough of low pressure moved through the Mid Atlantic while a weak clipper system moved through Central Virginia.  Cold temperatures and banding produced advisory level snowfall accumulations.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Snowfall amounts of around 1 inch were reported at the National Arboretum during the morning rush hour.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: High pressure was located across the Mid Atlantic and clear and calm conditions led to temperatures to fall below freezing.  This ended the growing season in many locations.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Temperatures of 32 degrees or less were reported at surrounding mesonets.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A cold front stalled across the Mid Atlantic and low pressure formed and moved along it. Southerly flow advected gulf moisture into the region and showers and thunderstorms easily formed. Heavy rain, hail, damaging winds and tornadoes occurred throughout the day.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: There was a tree down across 18th Street NW near P Street NW.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An upper level disturbance moved ahead of an approaching cold front. Southerly flow ahead of system led to increased moisture and warmer temperatures. There was enough instability to produce gusty winds, heavy rain and a tornado.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: There was a tree reported down blocking North Bound Lane of Beach Drive at Park Road.
